name: A Kerberos service ticket was requested
applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 R2 and later; Windows 7 and later
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4769 records a Kerberos service-ticket request on a domain controller and is central to detecting anomalous service access and Kerberoasting patterns.
trigger_scenarios: The event is logged whenever a client requests a ticket for a service principal name. A normal user session may generate several requests as it accesses file servers, web applications, and other Kerberos-enabled services.

key_fields.explanation: The user or computer account requesting the service ticket. Assess whether the requester is expected to access the target service.
The SPN for the requested service. Requests for many distinct or high-value service accounts can reveal enumeration or ticket collection.
The client IP address observed by the domain controller. It helps identify the source device conducting unusual service-ticket activity.
The encryption type used for the service ticket. RC4 requests for service accounts can be relevant when investigating Kerberoasting exposure.

false_positives: Application servers and middleware may request many service tickets during normal operation.
Inventory, backup, and monitoring accounts often access broad sets of services.
Legacy service accounts can legitimately use RC4 until the environment is remediated.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting

detection_notes: Look for a source requesting an unusual number of distinct SPNs, service tickets for accounts rarely used by that source, or RC4-encrypted tickets where AES is the norm. Maintain per-account and per-host baselines, then pivot from the Client Address to endpoint process telemetry and interactive logons.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4769
| summarize ServiceCount = dcount(ServiceName), Requests = count() by IpAddress, TargetAccount, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where ServiceCount > 20
| order by ServiceCount desc
spl_snippet:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4769
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats dc(Service_Name) as service_count count by _time, Client_Address, Account_Name
| where service_count > 20
sample_log:
Account Name: jsmith@CORP.EXAMPLE
Service Name: MSSQLSvc/sql01.corp.example:1433
Client Address: 10.x.x.x
Ticket Encryption Type: 0x12
Failure Code: 0x0
